engineGuide.h2: Volvo V40 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volvo V40 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Twin-turbo VEA diesel powering flagship D4 variants; high performance but timing chain concerns drive replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: D4 Momentum, D4 R-Design, D4 SE Lux, D4 Inscription, Cross Country D4
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - rattling noise and potential failure between 60k-100k miles
Inlet manifold melting - R/2019/180 recall on pre-2017 units
EGR cooler leak - coolant ingress causing overheating on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T14 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Single-turbo VEA diesel for mid-range D3 models; balanced performance and economy, EGR/DPF issues common.
compatibleVariants: D3 Kinetic, D3 Momentum, D3 R-Design, D3 SE Lux
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - limp mode and rough idle on short-journey vehicles
DPF regeneration failure - blocked filter causing warning lights and reduced performance
Turbo actuator sticking - loss of boost pressure after 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T9 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Early VEA diesel for entry-level D2; economical but susceptible to emissions system failures in urban use.
compatibleVariants: D2 Kinetic, D2 Momentum, D2 SE
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup causing rough idle and warning lights
DPF clogging - frequent regeneration failures on low-mileage urban cars
Coolant hose deterioration - R/2018/377 recall on early production units
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T8 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: PSA-sourced 1.6L diesel for early D2 models; reliable block but emissions components prone to failure.
commonFailures: EGR cooler leak - coolant mixing with exhaust gases causing overheating
Turbo oil feed pipe failure - oil leaks and turbo damage on high-mileage units
Timing belt tensioner wear - recommended replacement at 120k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4162T Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Petrol
entries.history: Three-cylinder Drive-E petrol for T3 models; efficient but balance shaft issues affect long-term reliability.
compatibleVariants: T3 Kinetic, T3 Momentum, T3 R-Design, T3 SE Lux
commonFailures: Balance shaft wear - vibration and oil consumption increase after 70k miles
Turbo actuator failure - reduced power and limp mode on urban-driven examples
Carbon buildup on intake valves - hesitation and rough idle on direct-injection units
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4154T4 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Four-cylinder Drive-E turbo petrol for T4 models; strong performance with generally good reliability record.
compatibleVariants: T4 Momentum, T4 R-Design, T4 SE Lux, T4 Inscription, Cross Country T4
commonFailures: Turbo wastegate sticking - boost pressure fluctuations and reduced performance
PCV system failure - oil consumption and blue smoke on high-mileage examples
Coolant thermostat housing leak - plastic housing cracks causing overheating
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T19 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L High-Output Petrol
entries.history: High-output Drive-E petrol for flagship T5 models; performance-focused with specialist rebuild requirements.
compatibleVariants: T5 R-Design, T5 SE Lux, T5 Inscription, Cross Country T5, T5 AWD Geartronic
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear - hard starting and reduced power after 80k miles
Turbocharger bearing failure - whining noise and oil consumption on performance-driven units
Timing chain guide wear - rattling noise on cold start after 100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T41 Volvo V40 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: Five-cylinder turbo petrol for early T5 models; characterful sound but complex turbo system drives replacement costs.
compatibleVariants: T5 (early production), Cross Country T5 (early production)
commonFailures: Turbo oil return pipe clogging - turbo failure and blue smoke on high-mileage units
Timing belt tensioner failure - recommended preventative replacement at 120k miles
PCV system blockage - oil leaks and increased consumption after 90k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for B5254T14 Volvo V40 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Volvo V40 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volvo V40's engine range is generally robust, but the VEA diesel D4204T14 and Drive-E petrol B4154T4 units dominate replacement enquiries due to timing chain and balance shaft issues. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.group: Volvo V40 D4 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Volvo V40 D4 Timing Chain Failure - Why D4204T14 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Volvo V40 D4, Cross Country D4 (2013-2019, D4204T14)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain stretch, causing rattling noise and potential valve timing loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides before catastrophic failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early; prevents engine damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage or failed units; addresses root cause

problems.recommendation: If your D4 V40 has under 80k miles and shows early rattling, a preventative chain replacement offers better value. For engines over 100k miles or with confirmed failure, a rebuilt D4204T14 with upgraded components is the most reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D4204T14 engine prices for your Volvo V40

problems.h4: Volvo V40 EGR & DPF Failure - Why D2/D3 Diesel Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.rootCause: Carbon buildup clogs EGR valve and DPF on short-journey urban driving, triggering limp mode and warning lights.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean or replace EGR valve, force DPF regeneration, update software
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage clogging; may recur on urban-use vehicles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated emissions components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage or repeatedly failing units; eliminates recurring costs

problems.recommendation: For first-time EGR/DPF warnings under 70k miles, cleaning offers good value. If your V40 has repeated limp mode episodes or exceeds 90k miles, a rebuilt diesel engine with updated components provides better long-term reliability and cost certainty.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Volvo V40 D2 D3 rebuilt diesel engines

problems.h4: Volvo V40 Turbo Failure - Boost Loss Across Diesel & Petrol Variants
problems.affectedModels: Volvo V40 D3, D4, T4, T5 (2013-2019, multiple codes)
problems.rootCause: Turbo actuator sticking, oil feed clogging, or bearing wear causes boost loss, whining noise, and reduced performance.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbocharger with reconditioned unit, update oil lines
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine block is healthy; may not address root oil-quality issues

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new/upgraded turbo, full gasket set, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage units or where turbo failure caused secondary damage

problems.recommendation: If turbo failure is isolated and the engine has good compression, a turbo-only replacement can be cost-effective. For V40s over 90k miles or with oil consumption issues, a full rebuilt engine addresses both the turbo and underlying wear factors.
problems.cta: Compare Volvo V40 turbo engine replacement prices

problems.h4: Volvo V40 Inlet Manifold Recall - R/2019/180 Compliance Replacement
problems.rootCause: Plastic inlet manifold cracks under heat stress, causing coolant leaks, overheating, and potential engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Free dealer recall fitment if eligible; otherwise specialist supply & fit
repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses manifold; does not repair any overheating damage already caused

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt D4204T14 with updated manifold, head check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if overheating caused head gasket or block damage; ensures full recall compliance

problems.recommendation: Check your VIN for recall eligibility first. If your V40 D4 has overheating symptoms or confirmed manifold damage, a rebuilt engine with updated components is the safest route to avoid repeat failures.
problems.cta: Check recall compliance for your Volvo V40 D4 engine

problems.h4: Volvo V40 Coolant Leak & Overheating - Head Gasket Risk Across Variants
problems.affectedModels: Volvo V40 D2, D3, D4, T3, T4 (2012-2019, multiple codes)
problems.rootCause: Plastic coolant hose/thermostat housing cracks or EGR cooler leaks cause coolant loss, leading to overheating and head gasket failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hoses, thermostat housing, pressure test system
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early leaks with no overheating damage; preventative maintenance

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with updated cooling components, head check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Required if overheating caused warped head or blown gasket; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: Address coolant leaks immediately to avoid costly head damage. If your V40 has overheated or shows white smoke/oil contamination, a rebuilt engine is the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Volvo V40 overheating engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volvo V40
finalCta.paragraph: From timing chain concerns on D4 diesels to balance shaft wear on T3 petrols, the Volvo V40's most common engine failures are well-documented and addressable. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volvo V40 specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components that target the original failure point. With nationwide delivery, supply & fit options, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, you can get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volvo V40 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volvo V40 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Volvo V40 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)

types.backDescription: Reconditioned Volvo V40 engines undergo thorough cleaning, measurement and parts replacement to restore reliable operation. Popular for D4204T14, B4154T4 and D4204T9 codes.

Best for V40s with under 100k miles and good service history

types.frontDescription: Targeted repair of specific failure points like EGR, turbo or timing components; cost-effective for known issues on otherwise healthy V40 engines.

knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and torque for loaded driving
Timing chain tensioner wear on D4204T14 between 60k-100k miles
EGR/DPF failures on urban-driven D2/D3 units triggering limp mode
High UK replacement demand on D4 variants due to performance popularity

items.title: Volvo V40 Petrol Engines

items.descriptor: Petrol V40 models (T2/T3/T4/T5) offer smoother refinement and lower emissions complexity, with Drive-E 1.5L/2.0L units dominating post-2015 production.

engineYears.h2: Volvo V40 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Volvo V40's UK production timeline (2012-2019), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.preview: Launch Generation: PSA Diesel & Legacy Petrol
years.description: The V40 launched with PSA-sourced 1.6L diesel and Volvo's legacy five-cylinder petrol, establishing the model's efficiency and performance credentials.
keyChanges: Volvo V40 model launch in UK market (2012)
Introduction of PSA DV6 1.6L diesel (D4162T) for D2 variants
Legacy B5254T 2.5L five-cylinder turbo petrol for early T5 models
Pre-facelift exterior and interior design language
knownFor: Strong initial sales driven by efficient D2 diesel pricing
PSA diesel reliability concerns emerging on high-mileage urban examples
Five-cylinder T5 praised for characterful performance but complex turbo system
Early production coolant hose issues leading to recall R/2018/377
years.cta: Get quotes for 2012-2014 Volvo V40 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo V40 D2 engine replacement • Volvo V40 1.6 diesel EGR fix • Volvo V40 T5 turbo replacement cost
enquiries: Volvo V40 T5 turbo replacement cost

years.preview: Facelift Introduction: VEA Diesel & Drive-E Petrol
years.description: The 2015 facelift introduced Volvo's modular VEA diesel and Drive-E petrol families, transforming the V40's engine lineup with improved efficiency and performance.
keyChanges: Introduction of VEA (Volvo Engine Architecture) diesel family (D4204T9/T14)
Launch of Drive-E petrol family (B4154T4, B4204T19) replacing legacy units
Exterior facelift with Thor's Hammer DRL signature
Updated infotainment and interior trim levels
knownFor: VEA D4204T14 praised for performance but timing chain concerns emerge
Drive-E B4154T4 balance shaft wear reports on early production T3 units
Improved fuel economy across both diesel and petrol ranges
Inlet manifold recall R/2019/180 affects pre-2017 D4204T14 units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2016 Volvo V40 replacement engines
years.ticker: Volvo V40 D4 timing chain replacement • Volvo V40 T3 balance shaft fix • Volvo V40 D4 inlet manifold recall
enquiries: Volvo V40 D4 timing chain replacement
Volvo V40 T3 balance shaft fix
Volvo V40 D4 inlet manifold recall

years.description: Final production years focused on quality refinement, recall compliance updates, and strong residual values driving replacement engine demand.
keyChanges: Updated software calibrations for VEA and Drive-E engines
Recall compliance updates for inlet manifold (R/2019/180) and fuel hose (R/2018/377)
Introduction of Cross Country variant with AWD powertrains
Final model year production and discontinuation announcement (2019)
mainEngines: D4204T14 - 2.0L diesel twin-turbo (D4, Cross Country D4)
B4204T19 - 2.0L petrol turbo (T4, Cross Country T4)
B4204T41 - 2.0L high-output petrol (T5, Cross Country T5)
knownFor: Strong residual values maintaining demand for quality rebuilt engines
Timing chain preventative replacement recommendations for D4204T14 over 80k miles
Cross Country AWD variants commanding premium replacement pricing
Final-year models benefiting from all recall updates and quality improvements
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Volvo V40 replacement engines

faq.h2: Volvo V40 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Volvo V40 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Volvo V40 engine prices range from £1,200 for entry petrol units to £4,800 for D4 diesel or T5 performance variants, supply only. Supply & fit adds £450-£850 labour depending on location and garage.
keyPoints: D2/D3 diesel rebuilt: £1,400-£4,200 supply only
T3/T4 petrol rebuilt: £1,200-£4,200 supply only
D4/T5 performance rebuilt: £3,200-£8,500 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £450-£850 to engine price
items.cta: Compare Volvo V40 engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most common Volvo V40 engine problem?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner wear on the D4204T14 D4 diesel is the most reported Volvo V40 engine failure, typically occurring between 60k-100k miles and causing rattling noise or catastrophic failure if ignored.
keyPoints: D4204T14 timing chain issues dominate V40 replacement enquiries
EGR/DPF clogging common on urban-driven D2/D3 diesel variants
Balance shaft wear affects early B4154T4 T3 petrol units after 70k miles
Inlet manifold recall R/2019/180 affects pre-2017 D4 models
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo V40 D4 timing chain replacement

items.question: How do I find my Volvo V40 engine code?
items.answer: Your Volvo V40 engine code is located on the engine block stamping, in the V5C logbook section D.2, or can be instantly verified by entering your registration number on EngineMarket for DVLA-matched identification.
keyPoints: Check V5C logbook section D.2 for factory engine code
Engine block stamping visible near gearbox housing on most V40 variants
DVLA registration lookup provides instant code verification
Common codes: D4204T14 (D4), B4154T4 (T3), B4204T19 (T4)
items.cta: Verify your Volvo V40 engine code instantly

items.question: Should I repair or replace my Volvo V40 engine?
items.answer: For Volvo V40 engines with confirmed catastrophic failure, low compression, or over 100k miles with major component wear, replacement with a rebuilt unit typically offers better long-term value than major repairs.
keyPoints: Rebuild cost often exceeds 70% of rebuilt engine price for major failures
Rebuilt engines include upgraded components addressing known failure points
12-month unlimited mileage warranty on rebuilt units vs limited repair warranty
Consider vehicle value: replacement makes sense for V40s worth £3,000+
items.cta: Get a Volvo V40 repair vs replace cost comparison

items.question: What warranty comes with Volvo V40 replacement engines?
items.answer: All Volvo V40 engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty as standard, with some specialists offering extended 24-month options at additional cost.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK garage for claim convenience
Extended 24-month options available from select premium rebuilders
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Compare Volvo V40 engine warranty options

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Volvo V40?
items.answer: Engine code swaps in the Volvo V40 are technically complex and may affect emissions compliance, insurance validity and MOT certification; we recommend matching the original factory code unless pursuing a specialist conversion.
keyPoints: ECU, wiring loom and transmission compatibility must be verified for any swap
Emissions compliance (Euro 5/6) must be maintained for MOT legality
Insurance providers must be notified of any engine modification
DVLA may require V5C amendment for significant engine changes
items.cta: Enquire about Volvo V40 engine compatibility verification

items.question: How long does Volvo V40 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Typical Volvo V40 engine replacement takes 1-2 days for supply-only delivery plus 1 day for garage fitting; supply & fit bundles from our network can complete the entire process in 2-3 working days.
keyPoints: Supply-only engines typically dispatched within 24-48 hours of order confirmation
Specialist garage fitting requires 6-8 hours labour for standard variants
Cross Country AWD variants may require additional time for drivetrain alignment
Post-installation testing and ECU adaptation adds 1-2 hours to fitting time
items.cta: Find Volvo V40 supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: Are Volvo V40 replacement engines reliable?
items.answer: Rebuilt Volvo V40 engines from EngineMarket's vetted specialists undergo rigorous testing and include upgraded components addressing known failure points, delivering reliability equal to or exceeding original factory specifications.
keyPoints: All engines bench-tested before dispatch with compression and leak-down verification
Upgraded timing components address D4204T14 chain tensioner concerns
Updated EGR/DPF components reduce recurrence of emissions system failures
12-month unlimited mileage warranty provides post-installation peace of mind
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Volvo V40 engine quality standards

items.question: Do you supply Volvo V40 engines nationwide UK?
items.answer: Yes, EngineMarket's network of 100+ UK Volvo V40 engine specialists offers nationwide delivery for supply-only orders and supply & fit services covering all mainland UK postcodes.
keyPoints: Nationwide courier delivery for supply-only engine orders (24-48 hour dispatch)
Supply & fit garage network covers all major UK cities and regions
Cross Country and AWD variants supported by specialists with appropriate equipment
Remote location surcharges may apply; confirmed during quote process
